h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I maybe get it: the “wrong” mark might be about the work standard, not proof the home sentence has no rules.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to hesitantly practice mapping the sentence while struggling to trust their own ear over the correction mark.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The gram example makes the distinction exact: the p-value shows detection, not weight.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue incorporating the tutor's step-by-step refinements to their poster's abstract.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: But now I think it is more like the court has to start there until the prosecution proves guilt, so it is not really a verdict about what happened.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with distinguishing procedural posture from factual innocence.
